title: Drone captures man surfing enormous sand dunes | USA TODAY #Shorts
creator: USA TODAY
notes: CANDIDATE-SOURCED, INDEPENDENTLY RE-VERIFIED THIS SESSION -- first exemplar added to the sand-dune/erg-desert-landscapes subgenre bucket, a subject not previously represented in this pack. A prior agent's candidate JSON proposed this URL (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=58os7tMxaYg); per this task's step-1 requirement it was NOT trusted verbatim -- it was re-verified live in this session via YouTube's oEmbed endpoint (https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=58os7tMxaYg&format=json), which returned title "Drone captures man surfing enormous sand dunes | USA TODAY #Shorts", author_name 'USA TODAY', and author_url 'https://www.youtube.com/@USATODAY' -- an exact field-for-field match to the candidate's claimed title, creator, and channel, so this session's own primary-source check corroborates rather than merely repeats the prior agent's claim. No Creative Commons marking of any kind was present in the oEmbed response, so per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise), license_category is set to 'all-rights-reserved' -- consistent with USA TODAY being a conventional news-broadcaster channel with standard copyright, not a CC-licensed uploader. Consequently local_media_path stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo. duration is left null: YouTube's oEmbed response does not carry a duration field, and no further verification of runtime (YouTube Data API call or manual playback) was attempted in this session -- rather than fabricate a plausible-looking number, this field stays unset. award_or_showcase_provenance is left null: this is a news-broadcaster repost of dramatic action footage (a person sandboarding down a large dune, shot from a drone), not a formally juried contest or festival selection, and no award claim was found anywhere in the oEmbed metadata or the prior agent's research -- inventing one would violate the no-fabrication constraint. Location note, carried forward with an honest confidence hedge: the prior agent's research placed the filming location near Iquique, Chile; a WebSearch run in this session returned a synthesized answer corroborating that same location, but none of the individual result links returned in this session directly cited Iquique for this specific video (they were mostly generic sandboarding-destination pages), so this location detail is treated as plausible and consistent with the prior agent's finding rather than independently confirmed against a primary source in this session. Subject-distinctiveness note (carried forward from the prior agent's verification_notes): this pick was included alongside an existing/candidate Utah-dunes pick as a genuinely distinct location, creator/outlet, and action-oriented visual style (a human sandboarding subject vs. a quiet nature reveal) rather than a redundant repeat -- this session did not itself re-verify that comparative claim about a second candidate (out of scope for a single-record task), so it is attributed to the prior agent's research rather than asserted as independently confirmed here. scores (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are scores_provenance: manually_estimated placeholder judgments, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the spec's no-bulk-download constraint. Calibration reasoning, consistent with this pack's existing ~0.70-0.85 range: sharpness=0.76 (a professional news-broadcaster repost implies a competent camera/drone platform and a standard broadcast-quality re-encode, comparable to this pack's other broadcaster-sourced exemplars such as abc-news-volcano-crater-lava-fpv.json's 0.77 and bbc-earth-aerial.json's 0.78, nudged only slightly below that tier since no explicit camera/lens model was confirmed for this specific clip); exposure=0.74 (bright, evenly sunlit desert/coastal-dune terrain in daylight is a comparatively favorable dynamic-range case -- large uniform sand surfaces generally avoid the harsh highlight/shadow clipping seen in this pack's backlit-sunset or molten-lava exemplars, e.g. lido-wave-nazare-coastal.json's 0.70 -- but scored just below this pack's top tier since bright reflective sand is still a documented source of localized highlight clipping if the sensor/exposure setting is not deliberately compensated, and no such compensation claim was found for this clip); motion_smoothness=0.72 (the footage is described, and the genre is consistent with, an active aerial tracking/pursuit shot following a fast-moving human subject down a dune face, which is a structurally harder smoothness case than a static reveal or a single deliberate FPV descent line -- keeping a moving subject framed typically requires more reactive panning/altitude correction, so this is scored below this pack's deliberate-descent exemplars such as drone-racing-league-waterfall-fpv-dive.json's 0.80 and abc-news-volcano-crater-lava-fpv.json's 0.78, and no explicit gimbal-stabilization-software claim, e.g. ReelSteady/Gyroflow, was found for this specific clip to offset that). composite_score IS a real computation: it was produced this session by calling this repo's own drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.76, exposure=0.74, motion_smoothness=0.72, composition=None) and drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights() via '.venv/bin/python3' in this session. default_weights() with no explicit version argument returned weights_version 'default-v2' (sharpness=exposure=motion_smoothness=composition=0.25 each); because composition's score is None (this pack's Milestone-1 Capability-1 convention), compute_composite_score's defensive None-skipping behavior excludes composition from both the weighted sum and the renormalization denominator, so the result reduces to the equal-weighted average of the three present signals: (0.76+0.74+0.72)/3 = 0.74 exactly, confirmed by actually running the function this session rather than hand-computed. editorial_style: a genuine, and notable, discrepancy was found in this session rather than a shortcut assertion of 'short-form-reel' from the title's '#Shorts' hashtag alone. Unlike this pack's abc-news-volcano-crater-lava-fpv.json precedent -- whose source URL used the native '/shorts/' path and whose oEmbed response returned portrait embed dimensions (height=200/width=113) corroborating vertical framing -- this video's source URL is a conventional '/watch?v=' path (not '/shorts/'), and this session's own oEmbed fetch returned landscape-oriented embed dimensions (height=113/width=200, a ~16:9 ratio), the opposite orientation from the ABC News precedent. This is a genuine counter-signal to the '#Shorts' branding in the title and to the prior agent's why_short_form_reel_style rationale (which relied on the hashtag/branding alone, not on structural verification), so 'short-form-reel' is deliberately NOT asserted here -- format stays null pending an actual playback review that could resolve the discrepancy. review_method is set to 'text_provenance_only' (this session's oEmbed fetch is a text/metadata check, not a live playback/scrub), and estimated_cut_count/avg_shot_length_seconds/transition_styles_observed stay null/empty rather than guessed, consistent with the prior agent's editorial-review pass (review_method 'not_reviewed', all fields null) finding no cut-count or shot-length detail in any source it consulted.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: No live playback or scrubbing of the video was attempted in this session (no browser-automation tool was invoked). This session's own YouTube oEmbed fetch found a structural discrepancy worth flagging: the source URL uses the conventional '/watch?v=' path (not the native '/shorts/' path), and the oEmbed response's embed dimensions are landscape-oriented (height=113/width=200, a ~16:9 ratio) -- the opposite of the portrait dimensions (height=200/width=113) this pack's abc-news-volcano-crater-lava-fpv.json exemplar obtained for a genuinely-vertical '/shorts/' URL. This means the '#Shorts' hashtag in this video's title is not corroborated by structural evidence obtained this session, so 'format' is left null rather than asserted as 'short-form-reel' from the hashtag/branding alone.
